description: §41.13 parseVariant (b) failure mode — MissingDiscriminator. The wire value is a JSON object with NO `tag` discriminator field ('{"foo":1}'). parseVariant raises ::ParseError::MissingDiscriminator and the exhaustive !{} handler routes to the MissingDiscriminator arm, which records @parseError = "missing-discriminator". The contract asserts WHICH ParseError variant fired (the author-visible outcome cell), not the impl-freedom error message text. domAnchored confirms the #status line renders the routed outcome.
rationale: §41.13 (Failure type / variant semantics): MissingDiscriminator — the JSON value has no `tag` field at parse time. Surfaced at runtime via E-PARSEVARIANT-DISCRIMINATOR-MISSING and, inside an !{} handler installed against the call, matched by the ::MissingDiscriminator arm. The call is failable and the handler's exhaustiveness over the four ParseError variants is required (§41.13; §19).
